The insertion of instrumentation into lower probe belts and assembly of vent and sterile gas filters can result in particulate and bioburden contamination of the vessel and system. The risk may be adequately mitigated by performing SIP on the system prior to use.2 A hot WFI rinsing and sanitizing rinse after manual insertions, but prior to SIP, may be needed to mitigate the risk of particulate contamination within the vessel chamber. Bioburden present in the system after human intervention is usually removed using thermal sanitization
